# NASA-style fixture sources

The `nasa-technical-report` project is a fictional interoperability fixture, not
an official template. Its structure is based on these public sources, checked on
2026-07-16:

- [NASA Publications Guide for Authors](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/citations/20050189209): describes recommended report organization and report-documentation forms.
- [NASA STI writing resources](https://sti.nasa.gov/writing-resources-and-style-guides/): identifies the U.S. Government Publishing Office Style Manual as the agency-preferred general style reference.
- [NPR 2200.2C, sections 2.6 and 2.12](https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/displayCA.cfm?Internal_ID=N_PR_2200_002C_&page_name=Chapter2): requires applicable STI standards and SI units for work performed in SI.
- [NASA units of measure reference](https://science.nasa.gov/learn/basics-of-space-flight/units/): provides SI names and symbols used in NASA science material.

The fixture translates those requirements into testable LaTeX characteristics:
front matter with a report identifier and disclaimer; abstract; contents;
symbols and acronym definitions; objective, scope, method, results, conclusions,
references, and appendix sections; SI quantities; numbered equations and tables;
and a final report-documentation page. NASA insignia, center names, real report
numbers, approval claims, and restricted-distribution markings are omitted.
